entry.title: Outbound process with SAP EWM: POSC, WOCR & Wave Management
entry.content:
Outbound logistics is a critical component of supply chain management, ensuring products are delivered to customers efficiently and accurately. In #SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), advanced tools like Process-Oriented Storage Control (POSC), Warehouse Order Creation Rules (WOCR), and Wave Management enable seamless execution of outbound processes. This blog provides an overview of a configured scenario to manage a complex outbound process, highlighting each component's role. 

Scenario Overview:

1. Customer Orders and Sales Order Creation:

  • Customer orders are received and processed in SAP.
  • A Sales Order is created in SAP S/4HANA based on customer requirements.
  • An Outbound Delivery is generated against the Sales Order in S/4HANA and distributed to SAP EWM as an Outbound Delivery Order (ODO).

2. Wave Management:

  • The actual EWM process begins upon the creation of the ODO.
  • In this scenario, Waves are created Automatically. A Wave groups multiple Outbound Delivery Orders
    based on specific criteria. For this case, the grouping condition is the Customer.
  • A Wave consolidates all ODOs associated with a single customer, facilitating streamlined operations.

3. Warehouse Order Creation Rules (WOCR):

  • WOCR is used to group Warehouse Tasks into Warehouse Orders based on predefined filters and limits.
  • It also supports the automatic creation of Pick Handling Units (Pick HUs) via packing profiles.
  • In this scenario, three WOCRs are configured for different resources:
    • Human Resources
    • Trolley
    • Forklift

4. Process-Oriented Storage Control (POSC):

  • POSC defines the sequential steps required to execute the outbound process.
  • In this scenario, the process includes:
  1. Picking: Pick Handling Units (HUs) are created during picking and moved to the packing work center.
  2. Packing: Products are packed by warehouse associates at the work center. Upon confirmation, an automatic Warehouse Task is created to move products to the Goods Issue (GI) staging area.
  3. Loading: Tasks are generated and confirmed to load products onto the delivery vehicle.
  4. Goods Issue: Final Goods Issue is performed, generating an Outbound Delivery Document in the system.

Upon Goods Issue, Post Processing Framework (PPF) actions are triggered to send the required information back to S/4HANA.

Configuration

This configuration focuses on the key steps, intentionally omitting basic configurations for the sake of brevity. It encompasses three critical components: Wave Management, WOCR, and POSC setup.

I.  Wave Management

As previously mentioned, this configuration involves auto wave creation using PPF Actions and PPF.

1. Maintain Wave Type
2. Maintain Wave Categories
3. Set Automatic Wave Generation for Warehouse Process Type
3.1 PPF Configuration

T.Code: SPPFCADM

Application

/SCDL/DELIVERY

Action Profile

/SCWM/PDO  01

Action Definition

/SCWM/PDO_01_WAVE

Method

/SCWM/WHR_WAVE_ASSIGN_NEW

Make sure Action Definition listed above active for AUTO Wave Generation.

3.2  Outbound Delivery Process Document Types
4. Define Condition Table
5. Define Access Sequence
6. Define Condition Types
7. Define Determination Procedure
8. Assign Procedure to Document type

9. Define Condition Maintenance Group
10. Maintain Wave Template

 
11. Maintain Wave Condition Record

II. Warehouse Order Creation Rule (WOCR)

In the context of WOCR, specific requirements have been defined based on the handling capacities of different resources:

  • WOC1: This rule applies to human resources where the load restriction is a maximum of 2 kilograms, suitable for items that can be carried manually by hand.
  • WOC2: This rule is designated for trolley-based operations, allowing a load capacity of up to 30 kilograms, making it ideal for medium-sized items that require mechanical assistance for movement.
  • WOC3: This rule is intended for heavy-duty equipment capable of managing loads up to 500 kilograms, suitable for large or bulky items that demand specialized handling equipment.

For detailed information and additional context regarding these requirements, please refer to the introduction section provided earlier. The basic configurations, such as number ranges for Warehouse Orders and sorting rules like "PIPA - Pick Path," have already been set up in the system. Let’s focus on the key steps involved in configuring WOCR.

1. Define Filters for warehouse order creation rule

 

2. Define Limits values for the size of a warehouse order

3. Define packing profile for Warehouse order Creation

3.1 Packaging Specification

Packaging specifications are a prerequisite for the packing profile. When creating a Pick HU, the system uses these specifications to determine the appropriate packaging material.

3.2. Assign Bins to Activity Area

Virtual bins are created for each activity area in advance and assigned accordingly. This step  is crucial for the creation of Pick HUs.

3.3 Warehouse process type for pick HU

3.4 Assign WPT to warehouse as WPT HU as Res

4. Define Creation Rule for warehouse Orders

5. Define Search Sequence of creation rules for activity areas

III. Process- Oriented Storage Control (POSC)

The POSC workflow involves the following steps: Upon the release of a wave, picking warehouse tasks are automatically generated, and pick HUs are simultaneously created. These pick HUs are then moved to the designated packing work center, where items are prepared for further processing. Following packing, the items are staged appropriately and subsequently loaded for shipment.

The Warehouse Process Type (WPT) "ZPIC" has already been configured in the system, and corresponding stock removal strategies have been established in alignment with the ZPIC WPT. These configurations ensure that stock removal processes are executed efficiently and adhere to the specific requirements associated with the defined WPT.

1. External Storage process

2. Define Storage process

3. Assign Storage process step

4. Process Oriented Storage Control

Assign storage process steps to their designated locations, such as the staging step to the storage type and the packing step to the corresponding work center.

IV. Work Center

The packing process requires a dedicated work center, for which the layout "VPMX" has been utilized. The standard storage type designated for the packing work center is 8030, and a corresponding bin has been created within the 8030-storage type to facilitate smooth packing operations.

1. Define Work Center

2. Assign Storage bin to Work Center

Test case:

Let’s consider a scenario where orders are received from customers. For instance, two customers place orders for three different products separately. The business requirement is to group these orders based on customers, which is referred to as a wave.

Once the wave is created, the WOCR comes into play. The configuration is set to be released immediately, warehouse tasks are automatically generated and grouped into warehouse orders based on the available resources. In this configuration, resources are categorized as Human, Trolley, and Forklift for picking operations. Additionally, Pick HUs are created automatically during this process.

Warehouse associates perform these tasks and confirm them using RF devices. The workflow then proceeds to move the picked products to the packing area, followed by staging and loading, and finally to the Goods Issue. Each subsequent task is created automatically once the previous task is confirmed which will be executed by the POSC. For simplicity, this blog demonstrates all processes using the Monitor instead of RF. In real-world scenarios, these processes are typically executed via RF in real time.

1. Sales Order Creation (VA01)

10 Sales Orders are created with two different customers for 3 products as shown in the table below. 24-2550(5Q) represents 24 is the Customer, 2550 is the products and 5Q in parenthesis is Quantity.

Sales Order

Customer-Products

Sales Order

Customer- Products

6127

24-2550(5Q)

6126

27-2565(2Q)

6132

24-2551(4Q)

6124

27-2550(30Q)

6133

24-2565(1Q)

6125

27-2551(2Q)

6130

24-2551(10Q)

6134

27-2565(1Q)

6128

24-2550(1Q)

6129

27-2550(2Q)

2. Outbound delivery creation in S/4 (VL01N)

Against Sales Order Created in the S/4, Inbound deliveries will be created, those are listed in the table below.

Sales Order

Inbound Delivery

Sales Order

Inbound Delivery

6124

80005863

6129

80005868

6125

80005864

6130

80005869

6126

80005865

6132

80005870

6127

80005866

6133

80005871

6128

80005867

6134

80005872

All outbound deliveries have been created and successfully distributed to EWM.

3. Outbound Delivery Order in EWM (/N/SCWM/PRDO)

Let’s check whether Outbound deliveries are created and distributed to EWM. Since 10 Outbound deliveries created, searched with advanced search.

Auto Wave Creation:

Waves can be created manually using T-code /N/SCWM/WAVE, but Auto Wave Creation enabled (refer the configuration step I. 3). This process starts with the WPT, triggering PPF actions to generate waves using wave templates 101 and 102, based on condition records configured earlier (refer configuration I. 11). The system created two waves: 5039 for Business Partner 148 and 5040 for Business Partner 129. The PPF log (see image) illustrates the wave creation process as per configuration (refer configuration step I. 3.1). Let’s get into the warehouse Monitor for wider visibility.

4. Wave Release (/N/SCWM/MON)

Two waves were created immediately upon delivery distribution, and they were automatically released. The wave status is marked as "R" (Released), and the wave release method is "B" (Immediate Release), as defined in the wave template. The configuration ensures that outbound delivery orders are grouped by the Ship-to Party, with one wave created for each customer. For instance, outbound deliveries for customer 129 were grouped into a single wave, as shown in the image.

The setup ensures that waves are automatically released for picking tasks as soon as they are created. Alternatively, manual wave release is also possible through the Warehouse Monitor or T-code /N/SCWM/WAVE, depending on the configuration in the wave template.

Warehouse Order Creation Rule (WOCR):

Next, let’s explore the steps following wave release by focusing on a single wave to understand how warehouse operations are executed. Once the wave is released, the system immediately refers to the Warehouse Order Creation Rules (WOCR) for creating warehouse orders.

In this example, three WOCRs were configured, and each was triggered based on the defined filters and weight limits. As shown in the image, the system created the following warehouse orders:WOC1: 2 kg, WOC2: Three orders of 5 kg, 5 kg, and 25 kg and WOC3: 150 kg.

These ranges are clearly defined in the WOCR configuration, where the link between the wave and WOCR is established through the wave category specified in the WOCR filters (refer to the configuration step- II.1).

Warehouse Task creation:

After WOCR processing, the focus shifts to the Warehouse Tasks assigned under the WO. These tasks are created to move stock from the picking storage type to the packing work center, as defined in the configuration.

This process follows the POSC configuration. As shown in the image, the WPT is ZPIC, with the Storage Process and External Step both triggered as ZPIC. The POSC is activated via WOCR, as the storage process is linked in the WOCR determination (refer to configuration step II.4). Picking operations are entirely driven by stock removal strategies, which play a crucial role in the creation of warehouse tasks for picking. However, detailed steps for stock removal strategies are omitted in this blog to maintain its brevity. It’s important to note that understanding these strategies is essential for efficient picking processes in the warehouse.

Let’s confirm picking warehouse tasks…

Auto Pick HU:

Upon confirmation of the warehouse tasks, the destination bin automatically changed to "VIRTUAL_BIN1". This behavior is linked to the Pick Handling Unit (Pick HU) configuration. The system is set to automatically create Pick HUs during the picking process, as defined in the WOCR Packing Profile (refer configuration step- II.3).

Based on the packaging specifications configured in the system, the system assigned the appropriate packaging material to each task. As shown in the image, a unique Pick HU number was created for each WT, ensuring proper handling and alignment with the configuration.

Next, let’s verify whether follow-up tasks were created to move the products from the VIRTUAL_BIN1 to the Packing Work Center for packing. To identify these subsequent tasks,  filter for Open WT with the WPT set to 3070, as specified in the POSC configuration (refer to configuration step III.4).

Subsequent tasks are created based on the external step "ZPAC" with WPT 3070, as configured in the POSC. Confirming these tasks moves the products to the Packing Work Center, which is linked to storage type 8030 and an assigned storage bin (refer to configuration step IV).

After confirming these tasks, no further tasks are generated immediately, as the system awaits a completion update from the work center. Once the system receives this update, it automatically creates the next set of tasks to move the products out of the work center. To update the completion status, the transaction code /N/SCWM/PACK is used.

Packing Work Center:

The "PACK" work center is configured as the designated Packing Work Center using the relevant T-code. In this interface, SAP provides a variety of features, including creating packages, repacking, and other functionalities, to pack products according to customer requirements.

Staging:

Upon completing the process step in the Packing Work Center, the system generates additional tasks with the external step "ZSTG", as illustrated in the image below.

These tasks are created to move the products from the Packing Work Center to the Goods Issue (GI) Staging Area. Let’s proceed to confirm these tasks as well.

Loading:

Once these tasks are confirmed, another set of tasks is generated to move the products from the Staging Area to the Door, completing the loading process. This marks the conclusion of the POSC workflow.

Let’s repeat the same process for the other wave, although the detailed steps are omitted in this blog for brevity. As mentioned earlier, in real-world scenarios, these tasks are not executed in the Monitor but are performed using RF devices. Finally, let’s check the status of the outbound deliveries in the PRDO transaction.

Goods Issue:

As shown in the image, the status of all outbound deliveries has been updated to Picking Completed, Packing Completed, and Loading Completed, indicating successful execution of the POSC process. By selecting Goods Issue, the system completes the goods issue for these outbound deliveries. Once the goods issue is completed, an outbound delivery document is created, triggering PPF actions to transfer this information to the ERP system.

Outbound delivery Document:

Outbound delivery Document created and PPF actions triggered to pass information to the ERP system as expected.

Let’s check whether the information passed to S/4..

T.code : VL03N-  S/4 Display Outbound Delivery

To illustrate the process,  verifying and displaying one of the outbound deliveries in this blog.

The Goods Issue information has been successfully updated in S/4HANA, as indicated by the Goods Movement Status marked as "C" (Completed). The system also triggered the Movement Type 601 in S/4, confirming the successful transfer of goods.

Conclusion

This blog explored the seamless execution of warehouse processes using Process-Oriented Storage Control (POSC) in SAP EWM. Starting with Auto Wave Creation & Release and Warehouse Order Creation Rules (WOCR), demonstrated how tasks are efficiently grouped and executed. The flow covered picking, packing, and goods movement from the Picking Storage Type to the Packing Work Center, Goods Issue Staging Area, and finally to the Door for loading.

Key configurations, such as Pick HUs and task automation, were highlighted, along with the integration of EWM and S/4HANA, where updates like Goods Movement Status and Movement Type 601 ensure real-time synchronization. While RF execution and repetitive steps were omitted for brevity, this blog showcased SAP EWM’s ability to optimize warehouse operations efficiently.

entry.title: Product Change Logs in EWM Warehouse Monitor
entry.content:
SAP Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA offers a feature to track and display product change logs directly within the Warehouse Monitor. This functionality enhances transparency by enabling users to monitor modifications made to warehouse products. Below is a step-by-step guide to activating and utilizing this feature.

Activating Change Document Recording:

By default, change document recording for warehouse products is not activated in the system. To enable this functionality, follow these steps:

We can activate the change document recording for the Warehouse product by following the below SPRO customizing path.

SPRO ---> SCM Extended Warehouse Management ----> Extended Warehouse Management ---->Master Data ----> Product -----> Activate Change Documents.On the activation screen, select the entry for "Product (Warehouse-Specific)" and click on "New Entries."Check both options to enable logging for changes in the "Warehouse Data" view and the "Storage Type Data" view.
Save your changes. These settings can also be transported to other systems.

Testing the Functionality:

To verify that the change document recording is active: Open transaction /SCWM/MAT1 (Maintain Warehouse Product).
Select a product, e.g., TG-100.
Make a change, such as modifying the process type determination indicator (e.g., changing from TG to RM).
Save the changes.

Viewing Product Change Logs:

Once the change document recording is active, you can view the logs using two methods:

1. Using Warehouse Monitor:

SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Master Data → Product → Display Change Documents.

Open transaction /SCWM/MON.
Select the node for "Product Master Data."
Enter the product details and execute.
Navigate to "More Methods" and choose "Display Change Documents." This will show all changes made at the warehouse product level2.Using Transaction Code /SCWM/PROD_CD_READ

Execute /SCWM/PROD_CD_READ.
Enter the following details:
User: Specify the user who made changes.
Date & Time: Define the range for tracking changes.
Product: Input the product code.
Warehouse Number and Party Entitled to Dispose.
Click "Execute" to display all logged changes for the specified product and timeframe.Benefits of Product Change Logs: 

Enhanced transparency and traceability of changes made to warehouse products.
Improved control over master data modifications.
Robust auditing capabilities for compliance purposes.
This feature is a valuable addition for organizations aiming to maintain accurate records of warehouse operations and ensure accountability within their EWM systems.

 

Reference SAP notes, 3485825 - Activation & display of Warehouse Product change documents

 

Best Regards,

Kannan Chokkanathan.

entry.title: Outbound process, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI
entry.content:
Outbound Process:

The outbound process in SAP EWM involves picking, packing, and shipping goods to fulfill customer orders. It begins with creating outbound deliveries and ends with goods issue, confirming that items have been dispatched. SAP EWM helps optimize the entire process by managing warehouse tasks efficiently.

Radio Frequency User Interface:

The Radio Frequency (RF) user interface provides warehouse workers with a mobile, handheld device to interact with the system in real-time. It enables efficient execution of tasks like picking, packing, and confirming activities with minimal manual effort. RF devices increase accuracy and speed by providing direct communication with the warehouse management system.

Picking:

Picking is the process of retrieving items from storage locations based on a customer order or warehouse request. The system can generate picking tasks and assign them to warehouse operators using RF devices, ensuring the correct quantity and items are picked. Various picking strategies (e.g., single or multi-order picking) can be configured for optimal efficiency.

Warehouse Order:

A warehouse order is a collection of tasks grouped together to optimize the warehouse worker's efficiency. It can include tasks for picking, packing, and other related activities. Warehouse orders streamline the execution process by organizing tasks into manageable units, helping to ensure faster and more accurate order fulfillment.

 

Steps to perform Outbound processes, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI 

Step1: 

Create Standard order using the Transaction code VA01

 

 

Step2: 

Create Outbound delivery:

 

Outbound delivery was distributed to EWM

 

 

Step3:

Created Warehouse task and warehouse order

Step4:

Performing picking by WO in RFUI

 

 

Provide the warehouse order number

 

 

 

 

The picking by warehouse order is confirmed.

 

Picking completed.

 

Conclusion: 

Steps to perform Outbound processes, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI is provided.

entry.title: SAP EWM - Multiple Auto Pick HU Creation for Single Warehouse Order
entry.content:
This blog shows demonstration of how individual Pick HU can be created for each Warehouse task in a single Warehouse Order. 

Business Scenario: For a Single Warehouse order with a multiple warehouse task, each warehouse task should have an individual Pick HU created automatically. 

Configuration:

  • Create WOCR (Warehouse Order Creation Rule)
  1. Creation of Limits: SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Define Limit Values for the Size of a Warehouse Order

2. Creation of Packing Profile: SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Define Packing Profile for Warehouse Order Creation

3. Creation of WO:  SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Define Creation Rules for Warehouse Orders

4. Determination of WOCR: SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Order >Define Search Sequence of Creation Rules for Activity Areas

  • Assignment of Pick HU Procedure

SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Define Warehouse Number Control

Select warehouse number and assign procedure.

Master Data:

  • Creation of Packaging Specification (/N/SCWM/PACKSPEC)
  1. Click on Create button and Select correct Packaging Specification group. ￼
  2. Enter the correct Packaging material
  3. Maintain condition records with a correct condition type.
  • Assign Start/End Storage Bin for Activity Area (/N/SCWM/SEBA)

 

Testing:

  • Create Sale Order (VA01)
  • Create Outbound Delivery (VL01N)

  • Open Outbound Delivery in EWM (/N/SCWM/PRDO)

  • Create Warehouse Task/ Warehouse Order

  • Open and check warehouse order for Created Pick Hus

               3 Warehouse tasks are created under one Warehouse order.

              We can observe Automatic Pick HUs are created for each individual Warehouse task.

We can observe that the destination HUs automatically assigned to each Warehouse task. We can confirm the warehouse task and take print of each HU label and stick HU labels.

  • Confirm Warehouse Order

                   Once all the warehouse tasks confirmed, WO will be confirmed.

entry.title: Kit To Stock in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Warehouse management is at the core of efficient supply chain operations, and SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) plays a pivotal role in streamlining these processes. Among its many features, the Kit-to-Stock process stands out as a crucial mechanism for assembling products (kits) from individual components and stocking them for future use. This blog explores the purpose, process, and benefits of Kit-to-Stock in SAP EWM, with a practical example to bring the concept to life.

The Kit-to-Stock process involves assembling a final product (referred to as the Kit Header) from individual materials or parts (called Kit Components) and storing the assembled kit in the warehouse. This ensures that the product is ready for future use, whether it’s for fulfilling customer orders or internal production needs.

A Practical Example

Let’s break this down using a real-world scenario:

  • Kit Header: EV Charging Port Assembly
  • Kit Components: Charging Port, Connector Cable

In this scenario, the EV Charging Port Assembly (Kit Header) is the finished product created by combining components such as the Charging Port and Connector Cable. These components are picked from the warehouse, assembled, and stocked as a single unit.

Benefits of the Kit-to-Stock Process

  • Operational Efficiency: The process saves time by pre-assembling kits, reducing the need to handle individual components repeatedly.
  • Inventory Accuracy: Real-time tracking of kits and components ensures precise inventory management.
  • Flexibility: The process adapts to various scenarios, including bulk kit assembly for high-demand periods.

The Kit-to-Stock process in SAP EWM is a game-changer for warehouse management. By streamlining the assembly and stocking of kits, it enhances efficiency, reduces errors, and ensures readiness for customer demands or internal requirements. Whether you’re assembling EV Charging Ports or any other kits, this process offers the flexibility and accuracy modern warehouses need to stay competitive.

Let’s dive into configuration to make this scenario work.

Configuration:

1.   Define Order Type for VAS for KIT to stock


2.   Define Storage type for Work Center

Create a bin (KITS_BIN1) in Transaction /N/SCWM/LS01 and generate an activity area (KITS) for the specified storage type using transaction /N/SCWM/GENA. Additionally, perform bin sorting for the activity area using /N/SCWM/SBST. These steps have been omitted from this blog to maintain brevity.

3.   Process- Oriented Storage Control (POSC)

The Process-Oriented Storage Control (POSC) in the kitting process is configured to manage both key aspects of the operation.

Inbound Process: POSC applies to the kitting header product, ensuring that products are moved from the kitting work center and put away into their final storage bin, making them available for sales.

Outbound Process: POSC is configured to manage the picking of kitting components from the storage area and transferring them to the kitting work center.

External Storage Process Steps

Storage Process- Definition

Assign Storage Process step

Process-Oriented Storage Control

4.   Work Center

A Work Center in the warehouse is a designated area for performing specific physical activities, replicated in SAP EWM to reflect the actual workflow. This setup allows the system to recognize the activities carried out at the Work Center, enabling warehouse associates to update information directly into the system.

In the context of the kitting process, this Work Center differs from a regular work center. Since the kitting process involves both inbound and outbound operations configured in the POSC, the storage type associated with this Work Center has the role “I - Work Center with Staging Area Group.”(Refer Step-2). This role is essential as it facilitates posting of the Goods Issue for consuming kitting components and the Goods Receipt for receiving the kitting header product.

Define Work Center

SAP provides a standard Work Center Layout, "KITS," which can be utilized for the Kitting Work Center. This layout will be assigned to the external step defined in the POSC configuration.

Assign bin to work center- /N/SCWM/TWORKST

5.   Warehouse Order Creation Rule (WOCR)

The WOCR is essential for triggering POSC during the outbound process. In this scenario, the WOCR configuration also enables the automatic creation of Pick HUs. While WOCR offers a range of functionalities, this specific scenario focuses on utilizing the Packing Profile to facilitate the automatic Pick HU process.

  • Define Packing Profile for Warehouse Order Creation

For Auto Pick HU, There are more configuration to be done, listed down the steps but screenshots are omitted. if you are interested please refer the following link for detailed Configuration on WOCR
Link: https://www.stelliteworks.com/warehouse-order-creation-rule-wocr-in-sap-ewm-for-picking/

  • Packaging Specification with “0BDL- Work Order Creation” in transaction /N/SCWM/PACKSPEC.( Refer Step-11)
  • Assign Additional Attributes to Activity Area in transaction /N/SCWM/SEBA.
  • Define Warehouse Process Type for Pick HU with Internal Movement.
  • Assign pick HU WPT in Warehouse Number Control.

Define Creation Rule for Warehouse Order

Assign Outbound Storage Process which helps in triggering POSC in Outbound Process and assign Packing profile for auto pick HU.

6.   Warehouse Process Type

Warehouse Process Types need both inbound and outbound side and this also is used to trigger POSC in Inbound process.

  • Define Warehouse Process Type

Determine Warehouse Process Type

7.   Define Put away Strategies for Header Product after Kitting

Put away Strategies to put the finished product after kitting.

  • Define Storage type search sequence for put away

  • Assign Storage type to the Storage type search Sequence

  • Define Put away Control Indicator

  • Specify Storage type search sequence for Put away

8.    Stock Removal Strategies

Stock Removal Strategies to pick the components from storage area to  Kitting Work Center

  • Define Storage type search sequence for stock removal

  • Define Control Indicator for Search Sequence
  • Specify Storage type search sequence for Put away

9.   Bill Of Materials (BOM)

 10.  Packaging Specification

Pack Specification detailed configuration is skipped in this blog.

 

11.   Product Master

Header:

Components:

 

The Kit-to-Stock process is an integral part of Value-Added Services (VAS) in SAP EWM. The process begins with the creation and maintenance of VAS for Kit-to-Stock.

Maintain VAS- Kit to Stock- /N/SCWM/VAS_KTS

SAP provides the flexibility to create kits with or without a Bill of Materials (BOM)

  • With BOM: Utilizes an existing BOM already configured in the system.
  • Without BOM: Enables the addition of components directly to the header product.

In cases where BOMs are used, they are created using CS01, as shown in Step 9. The configuration involves assigning the order type, packaging specification, and the header product along with its quantity. This setup allows the creation of the VAS for Kit-to-Stock, as illustrated in Steps 1, 10, and 11 respectively.

Outbound and Inbound Deliveries in the Kitting Process

The Kit-to-Stock process involves both Outbound and Inbound deliveries

  • Outbound Delivery: Created to pick the components required for kitting from storage bins and transfer them to the kitting work center.
  • Inbound Delivery: Created in the header details of the kit to facilitate the put away of the final product back into storage bins after the kitting process.

 

Outbound & Inbound Deliveries

Outbound Delivery- /N/SCWM/PRDO

The Outbound Delivery created manually as see indicator in the image and it includes two line items representing the components required to form the kit. These line items are configured with the warehouse process type "KTSO", as per Step 6. Outbound delivery is to create picking warehouse tasks for picking the components from storage bins and transferring them to the kitting work center.

 

  • Inbound Delivery- /N/SCWM/PRDI

Inbound Delivery (Warehouse Request) is automatically created to facilitate the movement of the final product, assembled through the kitting process, to the designated storage bin. This ensures the product is ready for sale and can be efficiently shipped when an order is placed.

The Inbound Delivery contains only one line item, representing the final product. This line item is created with the warehouse process type “KTSI”, as illustrated in the image below. This process ensures seamless integration of the kitting output into the warehouse workflow, enabling quick availability for shipping.

 Creating Picking Warehouse Task for components for kitting

To create warehouse tasks for picking manually, the system selects the products from the ET10 storage type, as shown in the image below. These products are available in the bin ET10-10-14-04, which belongs to the ET10 storage type. The picking process is driven by the Storage Type Search Sequence for stock removal, configured in Step 8, ensuring that the system efficiently identifies and retrieves the required products.

Upon confirmation of the warehouse task, the products are transferred to the kitting work center bin KTS_BIN1. However, the system generated an additional warehouse task due to the configuration in Step 5, where auto pick HU was enabled. To proceed, filter the warehouse tasks using the process type “3070” and open the warehouse tasks in the monitor due to the configuration in Step-3.

Only one warehouse task was found because both products were picked in the same pick HU and moved together to KTS_BIN1. Upon confirmation, the products are transferred to the work center as shown in the below image. At the work center, associates perform the kitting process and update the system to reflect the completion of the activity.

Confirmation of VAS Order (Kit To Stock)-/N/SCWM/VASEXEC

 The products, along with their bin and VAS order details, can be viewed in this transaction. Under the Post Components section, if the HU is posted using the Post HU button, the system will perform a goods issue for those products in the outbound delivery, indicating that the products have been consumed for the kitting process.

Now Let’s pack them in new HU for processing

The system navigates to the packing work center, where the final product is packed into a new HU, as shown below. After packing, save the changes, and upon returning to the previous screen, the newly packed HU will be listed in the kitting work center bin.

When the newly packed HU containing the final product is posted by clicking the Post HU button, a Goods Receipt is recorded, indicating that the final product has been successfully created in the system.

Next, click on “Complete Process Step for HU” and save the changes. The system will automatically create a warehouse task (52273) to move the product from the work center to the designated storage bin, initiating the put away process.

A warehouse task is created to move the product from KTS_BIN1 to the E101-04-03 bin. This bin serves as the final storage location for the kit header product. Upon confirmation, the put away process will also be completed in the inbound delivery.

Let’s check the bin for final product

Finally, the kit product is available for sale with stock type F2. When an order for this product is received, it can be directly picked from the designated bin and shipped.

entry.title: Automatic Generation of Packaging item for Goods issue S4 HANA
entry.content:
Introduction:-

This blog includes configuration and master data set up to achieve automatic generation of packing items in delivery document.

Business requirement :-

When business need to have visibility of what packaging material used in deliveries as well as how many quantity of packing material has been consumed to prepare shipment. then we have to perform below stated configurations and master data setup to achieve business requirement.

 

To achieve requirement below configuration has to be set up.

Configuration

1. Define Packaging material types

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Packaging Material Types for Shipping.

2. Define packaging material group for packing material.

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Material Group for Packaging Materials

3. Define Allowed packing material types.

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Allowed Packaging Materials

4. Define packaging material at EWM

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->Basics-->Define Packaging Material Types for Warehousing

5. Maintain Allowed Packaging Material Types for Packing Group

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->Basics-->Maintain Allowed Packaging Material Types for Packing Group

6. Assign number ranges for Packaging material

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->External Identification-->Assign Number Range Intervals to Packaging Material Types

7. Activate automatic generation of packaging items.

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Goods Issue Process-->Outbound Delivery-->Automatically Generate Packaging Items.

Activate Delivery item for combination of warehouse and Delivery type.

select warehouse line and double click on Relevance of Pack materials.

Select line and click on Determination of Delivery Item Type and maintain delivery type item type and stock type

in this configuration assign packing material to EWM document type and item type OPHU and select stock type as F2.

8. For ECC delivery type activate automatic packaging indicator.

SPRO-->Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Deliveries-->Define Delivery Types.

9. Assign packaging material  item categories to delivery type with usage of PACK.

SPRO-->Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Deliveries-->Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries.

Master Data set up

1. Create Packaging material and assign packaging material type and group in sales general/plant data view what we have created in above configuration

2. If Packaging material is inventory managed then goods movement bin should be maintained in transaction

/SCWM/GMBIN_DET.

Test case:-

  • Create sales order by using transaction Tcode VA01.
  • Create delivery with reference to sales order using Tcode VL01N.
  • Perform Picking activity in EWM
  • During Packing select packing material EP1RA-01 to perform packing in transaction /SCWM/PACK for EWM delivery.
  • Performing Goods issue from EWM deliver.

After Goods issue ODO contains packing material along with quantity

in ECC delivery also packaging material will appear

 

 

Conclusion:-

By Performing all above configuration and master data set up packaging material will start populating in EWM delivery as well as in ECC delivery with quantity which has been consumed perform packing in work-center.

entry.title: SAP EWM- Automatic Assortment Packing of HU in outbound delivery using UPB
entry.content:
This blog is intended to showcase how outbound delivery can be packed with an Assortment Packing (Mix HU) using the UPB (Unified Package Building).

Assortment Packing is packing of multiple different products into one package and mainly observed in Fashion industry. Also, in other industries such as Packaged sweets, Nuts & Candies, Cosmetics, Garment, Paint industry etc.

Business Scenario: User will pick material from bins of different storage types. After picking user will move products to Work center for packing. User expects system should pack products and create Ship HUs automatically. HU labels will be printed and pasted for shipping further.

  • Configuration

1) Creation of Packaging Specification

SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Define Condition TablesSCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Define Access SequencesSCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Define Condition TypesSCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Define Determination ProcedureSCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Create Condition Maintenance Group

2) Creation of UPB Profile (Unified Package Building)

SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Handling Units > Unified Package Building > Define Unified Package Building Profile

3) Assign UPB to Document Type

SCM EWM > EWM > Goods Issue Process > Outbound Delivery > Define Document Types for Outbound Delivery Process 

For Outbound delivery Doc type OUTB and doc type PDO. 

4) Work Center Configuration

SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Work Center >Specify Work Center Layout

For a Warehouse Work center layout (Eg. VPMX), Pack Automatically should be active.

  • Master Data 

1) Packaging Specification

Click on Create button and Select correct Packaging Specification group. Also Maintain condition records with a correct condition type.

If we are using reference packaging material, checkbox for Ref. Mix Allowed should be ticked. If there are batches which can be mixed checkbox Batch Mix Allowed should be ticked.

2) Reference Packaging material in Material Master

In S4 Material master in Basic Data 1 view or Sales: General/ Plant view maintain reference packaging material in which the product should be packed. It will be updated same in EWM product master.

  • Testing
  1.  Create Sale Order (VA01) 
  2. Create Outbound Delivery (VL01N)
  3. Open Outbound Delivery in EWM (/N/SCWM/PRDO)

  4. Create Warehouse Task/ Warehouse Order

  5. Open and Confirm Warehouse Order
    All the products are picked in a single Pick HU. Once all the warehouse tasks confirmed, WO will be confirmed.

  6. Go to Pack Workcenter (/N/SCWM/PACK)

    In Control Tab provide default bin. If we use POSC, then no need to enter bin manually.

    Select HU and click on pack button. Make sure Picking status should be completed. Based on Packaging specification Products are packed into different HUs. We can print the Handling Units labels and can be pasted on new Handling Units. 

  7. Once all the products are packed outbound delivery status changed to Completed.

entry.title: EWM Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) – A simple guide
entry.content:
This post will show you how to set up and test Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM). We'll focus on the customization and steps to follow the LOSC process. To start, let's imagine this scenario in an imaginary warehouse:



Step 1: Arrival at the Warehouse (Storage Type 9010)
Goods arrive at the warehouse dock and are unloaded into storage type 9010. They are scanned and entered the EWM system, starting their journey.

Step 2: Quality Inspection (Storage Type 0030)
Next, the goods move to storage type 0030 for quality inspection. Here, each item is checked to ensure it meets the required standards.

Step 3: Intermediate Storage (Storage Type 0035)
After passing the quality check, the goods are transferred to storage type 0035, an intermediate storage area. This is a temporary holding zone.

Step 4: Final Storage (Storage Type 0040)
Finally, the goods reach storage type 0040, the final storage area. Here, products are organized and stored until they are ready to be picked up and shipped to customers.


CUSTOMIZING STEPS

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Master Data -> Define Storage Types

Note: ensure that you have created the activity areas and storage bins for the storage types used in this process. Also, execute the bins sorting.

 

 

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Warehouse Process Type

Note: The WTP for "LOSC" was copied from 1010. Ensure the "Select HU w/o Storage Process" field is checked, as LOSC only works for handling unit warehouse tasks. If unchecked, a product warehouse task will be created, and the process won't work as intended.

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Control Indicators for Determining Warehouse Process Types

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Determine Warehouse Process Type

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Layout-Oriented Storage Process Control

 Note: In this scenario, we're using the same WPT for LOSC movements. For better traceability, you can create a WPT copied from 3030 to separate the LOSC steps.


TESTING
 

  • Step 1 – Goods receipt

    T-Code: MIGO

Inboud delivery created:

 

  • Step 2 – Inbound Delivery

T-Code: /SCWM/PRDI
Note: WT we’re created automatically using a PPF action but this is a subject to another post.


Note: HU we’re automatically created using packing specification.

 

  • Step 3 – Warehouse Management Monitor

T-Code: /SCWM/MON
Two warehouse tasks (WT) were created because we are using LOSC. The first warehouse task remains with status "B – Waiting":


The second warehouse task uses WPT "3030" to move the handling unit from 9010 to 0030 and has the status "Open":


Confirm the second WT:

A third warehouse task is created automatically when the second is confirmed:


The first WT has the source storage type and bin updated but remains with status "B – Waiting":


The third warehouse task is created to move the HU from 0030 to 0035:

Confirm the third WT:


The first WT has been updated and then the last move of HU is done:


Confirm the first WT:

Process done!


CONCLUSION

Using SAP EWM LOSC helps the warehouse efficiently manage the flow of goods through different storage types. This process ensures products are inspected, stored, and organized in the best way possible, leading to faster order fulfillment and higher customer satisfaction.

I try to make it simple, hope this guide helps you guys.

Best Regards,
Yuri Nogueira
SAP EWM Consultant

entry.title: Assessment with Logistics questions to support the explore phase (covering SAP EWM, TM, YL and BN4L)
